output:
(B).

The executive is committing **survivorship bias**. The 4.2 score is computed from a self-selected sample that systematically excludes the most dissatisfied customers — the ones who disengaged before churning. The metric doesn't measure customer satisfaction; it measures **satisfaction among customers who still bother responding**.

The 40% non-response rate among dissatisfied customers means the measurement system has a structural blind spot exactly where it matters most. The executive is treating a biased sample as representative.
